prevalence.source: Urological Society of India (USI) and ICMR
prevalence.trend: increasing due to extreme heat, dehydration, and dietary shifts

epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: USI guidelines: Ultrasound as primary imaging, non-contrast CT when available for confirmation, KUB X-ray for radio-opaque stones

publicHealthSystem.estimatedPatients: 70 million - very high burden in stone belt
publicHealthSystem.controlRate: 60% [with medical expulsive therapy and surgical interventions]

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: USI criteria, ultrasound primary in rural areas, CT in tertiary centers
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Conservative: increased hydration; Medical: tamsulosin for stone passage; Surgical: ESWL, ureteroscopy, PCNL; Ayurvedic: Cystone (Himalaya), Gokshura
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Stone belt with very high prevalence due to heat/dehydration, limited lithotripsy in rural areas, poor water quality, high recurrence rates
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: USI guidelines, growing lithotripsy access, Ayurvedic stone dissolution formulas (Cystone), affordable generic medications
